predicates.name: Distribution control: platform vs agent
predicates.statement: Distribution control over candidate-job discovery shifts to AI agents and external interfaces, vs remaining with platform UIs that own traffic origination and query routing.

predicates.name: Supply aggregation: horizontal vs vertical
predicates.statement: Supply aggregation favours horizontal cross-role liquidity over specialist vertical depth.

predicates.name: Candidate intent: implicit vs explicit-continuous
predicates.statement: Candidate intent becomes explicit and continuously updated (profiles, availability, preferences, real-time signals) rather than inferred implicitly from behaviour.

predicates.name: Matching: human-led vs model-led
predicates.statement: Matching, ranking, and recommendation become predominantly model-driven rather than human-filter-driven.

predicates.name: Verification: optional / required-centralized / embedded
predicates.statement: Verified data (identity, credentials, work rights, references) becomes required, and is delivered either via centralized verification services or embedded inside workflow tools, vs remaining optional.

predicates.name: Pricing: ads / hybrid / outcomes
predicates.statement: Pricing shifts from advertising-based monetisation toward outcome-based (per-connection, per-hire, SaaS) packaging, with hybrid models in between.

predicates.name: Hiring process: multi-step vs compressed
predicates.statement: The hiring process compresses (fewer steps, lower time-to-hire, more automation) vs remaining multi-step with manual handoffs.

predicates.name: Control point: marketplace / external interface / workflow
predicates.statement: The control point — where decisions, communications, and records live — sits with the marketplace, with an external interface (e.g. AI agent), or with the workflow / ATS layer inside the enterprise.

scenarios.name: Aggregator dominance
scenarios.description: Marketplace wins. Scale, trust, and discovery remain centralised on horizontal platforms; verification is required and centralised; ads-based monetisation persists; the marketplace is the control point.

scenarios.name: Agent-mediated market
scenarios.description: Interface layer captures discovery and routing. Distribution shifts to AI agents; intent becomes explicit and continuous; matching is model-led on the agent side; pricing is outcome-based; the control point sits with the external interface.

scenarios.name: Workflow / ATS dominance
scenarios.description: Value capture moves inside enterprise systems. The control point sits with the workflow / ATS; matching is model-led but embedded; verification is embedded in the workflow; pricing is outcome / SaaS; process is compressed.
